고린도전서 8:1.content: Now concerning things sacrificed to idols: We know that we all have knowledge. Knowledge puffs up, but love builds up.

고린도전서 8:2.content: But if anyone thinks that he knows anything, he doesn’t yet know as he ought to know.

고린도전서 8:3.content: But if anyone loves God, the same is known by him.

고린도전서 8:4.content: Therefore concerning the eating of things sacrificed to idols, we know that no idol is anything in the world, and that there is no other God but one.

고린도전서 8:5.content: For though there are things that are called “gods”, whether in the heavens or on earth; as there are many “gods” and many “lords”;

고린도전서 8:6.content: yet to us there is one God, the Father, of whom are all things, and we for him; and one Lord, Jesus Christ, through whom are all things, and we live through him.

고린도전서 8:7.content: However, that knowledge isn’t in all men. But some, with consciousness of the idol until now, eat as of a thing sacrificed to an idol, and their conscience, being weak, is defiled.

고린도전서 8:8.content: But food will not commend us to God. For neither, if we don’t eat, are we the worse; nor, if we eat, are we the better.

고린도전서 8:9.content: But be careful that by no means does this liberty of yours become a stumbling block to the weak.

고린도전서 8:10.content: For if a man sees you who have knowledge sitting in an idol’s temple, won’t his conscience, if he is weak, be emboldened to eat things sacrificed to idols?

고린도전서 8:11.content: And through your knowledge, he who is weak perishes, the brother for whose sake Christ died.

고린도전서 8:12.content: Thus, sinning against the brothers, and wounding their conscience when it is weak, you sin against Christ.

고린도전서 8:13.content: Therefore if food causes my brother to stumble, I will eat no meat forever more, that I don’t cause my brother to stumble.
